key_findings: EVAL 3 FIXED: Updated assertion now correctly passes the with-skill variant's architectural decision. With-skill scores 100% base + 100% quality (9/9 + 2/2). Without-skill scores 100% base + 0% quality (9/9 + 0/2).

EVAL 1 NEW ISSUE: The 'When NOT to add profileContactInfoPages' guidance now also triggers on new-chapter-36-form because that form also uses custom field names (claimantAddress, veteranAddress). This is architecturally correct but fails eval 1's strict assertion.

EVAL 2 STABLE: Both variants score 100% base. Quality bonuses are misaligned with this scenario — the 995 transformer needs EXTENSION (case 2), not the 'leave unchanged + dataAdapter' approach the bonuses reward (case 1).
QUALITY SCORING WORKS: Quality bonuses successfully differentiate with-skill (60% avg) from without-skill (33% avg) where base scores are tied. The +26.7% quality delta is the key skill advantage.
recommendations: Update eval 1 assertion to match eval 3 pattern: 'Adds profileContactInfoPages() to formConfig chapters OR explains why it should not be added' — the new-chapter-36-form genuinely has custom field names

Revise eval 2 quality bonuses to match the actual scenario: reward EXTENDING the transformer while preserving existing fields, rather than rewarding 'leave unchanged + dataAdapter' which doesn't apply here

Consider adding an eval 4 targeting a form where the existing transformer already has ssn/vaFileNumber/fullName at nested paths — this would properly test the skill's case 1 (do nothing + dataAdapter) guidance
The skill's 'When NOT to add profileContactInfoPages' guidance is working correctly but aggressively — it catches ALL forms with custom field names. This is the right behavior for production safety.
